'''''UFC 132: Cruz vs. Faber''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event held by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] on July 2, 2011, at the [[MGM Grand Garden Arena]] in the [[Las Vegas]] suburb of [[Paradise, Nevada]].<ref name="UFC 132">{{cite web |url=http://www.ufc.com/event/UFC132#/fight|title=UFC 132: Cruz vs. Faber|publisher=ufc.com|access-date=2016-04-04}}</ref>

==Background==
UFC 132 was originally set to feature a rematch between [[B.J. Penn]] and [[Jon Fitch]], but both were forced to withdraw because of injuries.<ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.mmamania.com/2011/3/30/2081711/jon-fitch-injured-out-of-ufc-132-fight-against-bj-penn|title=Jon Fitch injured, out of UFC 132 fight against BJ Penn |work=MMAmania.com |first=Geno |last=Mrosko |date=March 30, 2011|access-date=July 2, 2011}}</ref>

[[Evan Dunham]] was expected to face [[George Sotiropoulos]] at the event,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmamania.com/2011/3/17/2055792/ufc-132-evan-dunham-vs-george-sotiropoulos-booked-for-fourth-of-july|title=UFC 132: Evan Dunham vs George Sotiropoulos booked for Fourth of July weekend event |publisher=MMAmania.com|date=March 17, 2011 |last=Mrosko |first=Geno |access-date=June 10, 2011}}</ref> but was forced off the card with an injury and replaced by [[Rafael dos Anjos]].<ref name="DunhamOut">{{cite web |url=http://www.bloodyelbow.com/2011/5/3/2151948/evan-dunham-injured-out-of-ufc-132-fight-against-george-sotiropoulos |title=Evan Dunham Injured, Rafael dos Anjos Now Faces George Sotiropoulos at UFC 132 |publisher=bloodyelbow.com |date=May 3, 2011 |last=Haber |first=Scott |access-date=June 10, 2011 |archive-date=November 9, 2011 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111109092006/http://www.bloodyelbow.com/2011/5/3/2151948/evan-dunham-injured-out-of-ufc-132-fight-against-george-sotiropoulos |url-status=dead }}</ref>

[[Jason Miller (fighter)|Jason Miller]] was scheduled to face [[Aaron Simpson (fighter)|Aaron Simpson]] at this event.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmafighting.com/fight-card/315/ufc-132-cruz-vs-faber-2|title=UFC 132: Fight Card Results |work=[[MMAfighting.com]] |date=July 25, 2011 |last=Samuels |first=Albert |access-date=July 25, 2011}}</ref> However, Miller was removed from the card after accepting a coaching position on [[The Ultimate Fighter 14]], and replaced by [[Brad Tavares]].<ref name="Tavares">{{cite web|url=http://www.mmamania.com/2011/5/31/2198462/ufc-132-aaron-simpson-vs-brad-tavares-fight-booked-for-july-2-in-las|title=UFC 132: Aaron Simpson vs Brad Tavares fight booked for July 2 in Las Vegas  |publisher=mmamania.com|date=May 31, 2011 |last=Mrosko |first=Geno |access-date=June 10, 2011}}</ref>

[[Cub Swanson]] was expected to face [[Erik Koch]] at this event, but was forced from the bout with an injury.<ref name="CubOut">{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2011/06/training-injury-forces-cub-swanson-out-of-ufc-132-erik-koch-awaits-new-foe|title=Training injury forces Cub Swanson out of UFC 132, Erik Koch awaits new foe |work=[[MMAjunkie.com]]|date=June 2, 2011 |access-date=June 10, 2011}}</ref> Koch was instead moved to [[UFC Fight Night 25]] to take on [[Jonathan Brookins]].<ref name="UFC FN 25 Updates">{{cite web |url=http://www.ufc.com/news/ufc-ufn25-battle-on-the-bayou-fight-card-updates|title=UFC FN 25 Updates|publisher=ufc.com|access-date=2011-05-26}}</ref>

UFC 132 featured two preliminary fights live on [[Spike TV]]<ref name="Spike">{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2011/05/spike-tv-to-air-ufc-132-ufc-prelims-special-in-july|title=Spike TV to air UFC 132 "UFC Prelims" special in July |work=[[MMAjunkie.com]]|date=May 10, 2011 |last=Stupp |first=Dann |access-date=June 10, 2011}}</ref> and the remainder of the preliminary bouts streamed on [[Facebook]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.fightofthenight.com/news/ufc-132-all-six-prelims-air-via-spike-tv-and-facebook/|title=UFC 132: All Six Prelims Air Via Spike TV And Facebook|publisher=fightofthenight.com|date=June 28, 2011}}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $75,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://mmajunkie.com/2011/07/ufc-132-bonuses-condit-ortiz-cruz-faber-get-75000-awards|title=UFC 132 bonuses: Condit, Ortiz, Cruz and Faber get $75,000 awards|work=[[MMAjunkie.com]]|date=July 3, 2011 |last1=Morgan |first1=John |last2=Stupp |first2=Dann |access-date=July 3, 2011}}</ref>

* '''Fight of the Night: Dominick Cruz vs. Urijah Faber'''
* '''Knockout of the Night: Carlos Condit'''
* '''Submission of the Night: Tito Ortiz'''

==Reported payout==
The following is the reported payout to the fighters as reported to the [[Nevada State Athletic Commission]]. It does not include sponsor money or "locker room" bonuses often given by the UFC and also do not include the UFC's traditional "fight night" bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.mmamania.com/2011/7/5/2259445/ufc-132-payouts-salaries-and-earnings-for-cruz-vs-faber-2-event|title=UFC 132 payouts, salaries and earnings for 'Cruz vs Faber' event fighters |work=MMAMania.com|date=July 5, 2011 |last=Holland |first=Jesse |access-date=July 6, 2011}}</ref>

*'''Dominick Cruz:''' $40,000 ($20,000 win bonus) def. Urijah Faber: $32,000
*'''Chris Leben:''' $92,000 ($46,000 win bonus) def. Wanderlei Silva: $200,000
*'''Dennis Siver:''' $50,000 ($25,000 win bonus) def. Matt Wiman: $18,000
*'''Tito Ortiz:''' $450,000 (no win bonus) def. Ryan Bader: $20,000
*'''Carlos Condit:''' $68,000 ($34,000 win bonus) def. Dong Hyun Kim: $41,000
*'''Melvin Guillard:''' $64,000 ($32,000 win bonus) def. Shane Roller: $21,000
*'''Rafael dos Anjos:''' $28,000 ($14,000 win bonus) def. George Sotiropoulos: $15,000
*'''Brian Bowles:''' $34,000 ($17,000 win bonus) def. Takeya Mizugaki: $12,000
*'''Aaron Simpson:''' $34,000 ($17,000 win bonus) def. Brad Tavares: $10,000
*'''Anthony Njokuani:''' $16,000 ($8,000 win bonus) def. Andre Winner: $14,000
*'''Jeff Hougland:''' $12,000 ($6,000 win bonus) def. Donny Walker: $6,000
